responses.factual: The capital of France is Paris. Paris is a city in the country of France.
responses.narrative: Once upon a time, there was a young girl who lived in a small village near the forest.
responses.procedural: How do you make a cup of tea? You need: water, a tea bag. First, boil the water. Then add the tea bag.

responses.factual: The capital of France is Paris, which is also the country's largest city and cultural centre.
responses.narrative: Once upon a time, there was a clever merchant who could speak to animals. One morning, a fox knocked on his door.
responses.procedural: To make a cup of tea: 1. Boil fresh water. 2. Warm the teapot. 3. Add one tea bag per cup. 4. Pour boiling water and steep for 3–5 minutes.

responses.factual: The capital of France is Paris. It sits on the River Seine and has been France's political and cultural capital since the 10th century.
responses.narrative: Once upon a time, there was a clockmaker who had built every clock in the city — except one. The great clock in the town square had never worked, and nobody knew why.
responses.procedural:
To make a perfect cup of tea:
1. Use freshly drawn cold water.
2. Bring to a rolling boil.
3. Warm your cup or pot first.
4. Add your tea (1 teabag or 1 tsp loose leaf per cup).
5. Pour the water just off the boil.
6. Steep for 3–5 minutes to taste.
7. Remove the tea and add milk if desired.

responses.factual: The capital of France is Paris (population ~2.1 million city proper, ~12 million metro). Founded by the Celtic Parisii tribe around 250 BC, it became the French capital in 987 AD and today serves as the country's political, economic, and cultural hub.
responses.narrative: Once upon a time, there was a lighthouse keeper named Mira who had tended the Greywhistle Light for thirty years without once seeing a ship run aground. Then, on the night of the autumn equinox, she spotted a vessel sailing directly toward the rocks — with its lights off.
responses.procedural:
How to make a great cup of tea:

You'll need: fresh cold water, a kettle, your preferred tea, a mug or pot, and milk or lemon if desired.

Method:
1. Fill the kettle with fresh cold water — reboiled water tastes flat.
2. Bring to a full boil for black tea; 80°C for green tea.
3. Pre-warm your mug by rinsing it with hot water.
4. Place one teabag (or 1 heaped tsp loose leaf) per 200ml.
5. Pour water over the tea immediately.
6. Steep 3–5 min for black tea; 2–3 min for green.
7. Remove tea without squeezing (avoids bitterness).
8. Add milk, sugar, or lemon to taste.

responses.factual: Paris is the capital and largest city of France. Located in north-central France along the Seine River, it has served as the nation's capital since the late 10th century. With a metropolitan population of approximately 12 million, Paris is one of Europe's largest urban areas and a global centre of art, fashion, gastronomy, and culture. Landmarks include the Eiffel Tower, the Louvre, Notre-Dame Cathedral, and the Arc de Triomphe.
responses.narrative: Once upon a time, there was a mapmaker who charted every road, river, and mountain in the kingdom — except the valley that appeared only at dawn on the winter solstice. For years she had tried to reach it in time, but the roads always seemed to lengthen, the miles to multiply. Then, the winter she turned sixty, she finally understood: the valley wasn't hiding from her maps. Her maps had been hiding from the valley.
responses.procedural:
How to make the perfect cup of tea — a complete guide:

Choosing your tea:
• Black tea (English Breakfast, Darjeeling, Assam) — bold, malty, takes milk well
• Green tea (Sencha, Gunpowder) — grassy, delicate, no milk
• Herbal tisanes — no caffeine, huge variety

Water temperature matters:
• Black tea: full boil (100°C)
• Green/white tea: 75–85°C (let boiled water cool 2–3 min)

Steeping time:
• Black: 3–5 minutes • Green: 2–3 minutes • Herbal: 5–7 minutes

The method:
1. Start with cold, fresh water in the kettle.
2. Heat to the correct temperature for your tea.
3. Pre-warm your teapot or mug.
4. Measure: 1 tsp loose leaf or 1 bag per cup.
5. Pour water over the leaves/bag.
6. Cover and steep (timing above).
7. Remove tea; never squeeze a teabag.
8. Serve immediately. Black tea traditionally: milk first in the cup, then tea.

scaleComparisons.1000: At 1,000 batches, a real model would have processed roughly 32 million tokens — about 500 novels.
scaleComparisons.100000: At 100,000 batches, a real model would have processed ~3.2 billion tokens — equivalent to reading the entire English Wikipedia about 10 times over.
scaleComparisons.1000000: At 1 million batches, a real model would have processed ~32 billion tokens — more text than all books published in English in the last 100 years.
scaleComparisons.1000000000: At 1 billion batches, a real model would have processed ~32 trillion tokens — equivalent to reading the entire internet several times over. GPT-3 was trained on roughly 300 billion tokens; modern frontier models exceed 10 trillion.
